intro: Sterculia rupestris, also known as the rock sterculia, is a plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae, characterized by its unique, small flowers and edible seeds. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Sterculia rupestris include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the seeds of Sterculia rupestris are commonly used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are believed to promote digestive health and relieve symptoms of fever and inflammation.
